item.title: Experiments in Blender (2026)
item.description: No Dogs Allowed In Lake “NO DOGS ALLOWED IN LAKE” you notice casted on the ground It sure is effective - no signs of life around You wonder if that’d be different, would it not be for the five monoithic steel pillars erected there last summer. ‘No Dogs Allowed In Lake’ was my first experimentation of using multiple media and techniques to create an overarching narrative. The original photo, captured in The Common (Southampton) felt like an interesting base as it captured the signage on the floor while allowing a lot of space for addition.

item.title: Supersapient (2020-)
item.description: A foreword Supersapient was and still is an incredibly personal project for me, and it has proved really difficult to document it retrospectively. What at the time was a small game that I started with a few friends in the summer between our A-Levels and University had grown into an amorphous entity of massive proportions. I see it as a labour of love for the art of game development and all the media and styles that influenced it, and it holds so much meaning for me personally that it was ever intended to.

item.title: Synesthesia and Lyric Videos
item.description: A strange word Synesthesia is a phenomenon which links two sensory experiences together, such as hearing and vision, or vision and touch. A recognisable example is associating certain letters and numbers with particular colours. I have a mixed art/science background, and I find synesthesia to be a deeply fascinating subject. Nothing peaks my curiosity more than the inner workings of the brain, especially when it happens to project imagery, sounds, or other qualia by its own will.

item.title: Blue Corridors (2025)
item.description: Click on an image to cycle through the gallery - includes a full walk-through video of the finished app! Overview Originally developed for the 2025 ‘Whales’ exhibition at Winchester Cathedral, BLUE CORRIDORS is an interactive AR web-app based on a collaborative report between the WWF, University of Southampton, and 20 other institutions and charities. The app raises awareness of “Blue Corridors” - a collection of vital whale migration paths throughout the world, visualising the paths and highlighting human-engineered threats which disturb them.

item.title: Ozone (2025)
item.description: Ozone is an anti-game created for the 2025 Global Game over the course of 3 days. Presenting itself as a regular incremental game such as Cookie Clicker, it aims to criticise capitalist greed by subverting the expectation of infinite resources that are commonly assumed in similar games. Your browser doesn't support embedded videos, but don't worry, you can download it and watch it with your favorite video player! Objective: Accumulate as many bubbles (BUPS) as possible.

item.title: Mirrors (Installation Concept)
item.description: Proposal This is a concept of an installation that I would like to explore. Temporary name “Mirrors”, the audience is invited into a dark room, encouraged to go in by themselves or with their loved one. They see a waving sheet in front of them, on which a glowing pattern is projected. They hear ambient and atmospheric music emanating behind it. As they walk closer, a glowing figure fades in - a mirror image of themselves.

item.title: TouchDesigner
item.description: TouchDesigner is an incredibly powerful tool which allows for creation of real-time interactive and responsive content. I have begun experimenting with TouchDesigner in 2024 when asked to create some demos for our BSc Creative Computing course. The following is my collection of demos created in TouchDesigner: AR Visualisers Your browser doesn't support embedded videos, but don't worry, you can download it and watch it with your favorite video player! Your browser doesn't support embedded videos, but don't worry, you can download it and watch it with your favorite video player!

item.title: Undergraduate Research Project (2023)
item.description: Exploring the extent to which player modelling and personalisation impact the capacity of video games for the development of emotional intelligence (2023) Click here to view the full report (opens in new tab) Abstract Video games offer unique affordances for engagement not seen in other media. This report summarises existing literature in the spaces of player modelling, emotion theory, personalisation and emotional intelligence to provide a conceptual link between these four fields and assesses the effect that personalisation has on the potential of video games as a tool for prolonged emotional regulation.
